free_text: Excitation Functions of Reactions from d+Ti, d+Mo,
p+Ti and p+Mo
HI-13 tandem accelerator
The incident particle beam adjusted to about 4 mm in
diameter was focused on the center of the samples.
Typical beam currents were about 200 nA, and total
collected charge was about 1200 uC.
The natural metallic foils of Ti and Mo were rolled
and machined into the shape of 22 mm in diameter and
6 to 20 mg/cm2 in thickness. Three stacks were
irradiated with different primary energy for each
reaction, a stack consisted of 4 to 6 foils and
several Al foils used as energy degraders. One V foil
was placed in front of each stack to monitor the
intensity of incident beam.
The activities of the y rays for each product
were measured by gamma ray spectrometry using Ge(Li)
detector coupled to 919 analyzer and 486 computer
Conventional stacked foil method
The incident particle beam determined by a
Faraday cup and ORTEC mode 439 digital current
integrator.
The total uncertainty.
For statistics.
Efficiency calibration, 1.5%.
Weight and uniformity of sample.
Activity determination.
Intensity of incident beam measurement, 1.5%.
Correction of product nuclide recoil, 1.5%.
Data taken from Table 2 of INDC(CPR)-44,17,1998
Jimin Wang
